description_long: This guard rail features a classic balustrade design with six evenly spaced, turned balusters supporting a rectangular top rail. At one end, a prominent square post with ornate floral relief detailing provides structural and decorative emphasis, topped by a flat, square cap. The base of the rail and post is broad and flat, offering stability and a traditional architectural appearance. The entire structure is rendered in a uniform, light-colored material, likely painted or coated for a smooth finish. The balusters are symmetrically shaped, with rounded and tapered profiles, and the post's decorative panel adds visual interest. This type of guard rail is commonly found in urban parks, public gardens, or as a boundary on pedestrian walkways.
description: A decorative guard rail with six turned balusters, a detailed square end post with floral relief, and a smooth, light-colored finish. The design is traditional and ornamental, suitable for urban parks or walkways.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the guard rail, all six balusters, and the ornate end post with its decorative panel.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the square post's floral relief and cap, with the rail extending away from the viewer.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the balusters and the decorative post from the other direction.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizing the flat cap of the post and the alignment of the balusters along the base.
